context: Tests whether the agent writes a summary following Tessl conventions: keywords embedded inline with 'Keywords: ...' label (not a separate array), includes a 'Use when...' trigger phrase, appropriate length, and starts with an action verb. The naive approach would list keywords in a separate JSON array or write a vague one-sentence description.

checklist.name: Keywords embedded inline
checklist.description: Summary string contains keywords embedded inline (e.g., 'Keywords: aws, cost-explorer, billing...') — not stored in a separate JSON field

checklist.name: Keywords label present
checklist.description: Summary text contains 'Keywords:' followed by comma-separated terms within the summary string itself

checklist.name: Use when trigger phrase
checklist.description: Summary contains a 'Use when' phrase describing the trigger conditions for using the skill

checklist.name: Summary length 150-300 chars
checklist.description: Summary string is between 150 and 300 characters long

checklist.name: Starts with action verb
checklist.description: Summary begins with an action verb (e.g., 'Analyze', 'Identify', 'Optimize', 'Detect')

checklist.name: No trailing period
checklist.description: Summary string does NOT end with a period
